sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten letter from Oppel to Schenker, undated, ca. September 16, 1930
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: Oppel reports that the new teaching year is about to begin, and that he has a bad crop of new students. – He is revising and orchestrating his Passacaglia and playing new pieces by his colleague Günther Raphael.

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Professor Schenker!,Receipt of this letter is recorded in Schenker’s diary for September 18, 1930: “Von Oppel Noten” (“From Oppel, music”). See footnotes 2 and 4 for the editorial dating. — Paragraph-breaks are editorial, guided by emdashes in the original text and changes of topic.

tei:div.tei:p: hier schicke ich Ihnen die 6 Klavierstücke für meine Frau und Ulrich, bitte, verwenden Sie aber nicht mehr wie ¼ Stunde darauf. No 6 mit besonderem Gruss an Ihre liebe Frau. –

Der Betrieb beginnt richtig erst morgen, das Material, das neu kam an Schülern, taugt nicht viel. – Vorläufig ist das Tagesgespräch der Ausfall der Wahlen.Federal elections took place in Germany on Monday, September 15, 1930. The National Socialists (Nazis) won the second-highest number of votes, with a ninefold increase in their representation at the Reichstag (from 15 to 107 seats). –

Nachher beginne ich mit der Instrumentation und Ausfeilung der Passac. – Ich sah u. spielte neue Stücke von meinem Kollegen Raphael,Günther Raphael (1903–60), composer and violist, taught at the Leipzig Conservatory from 1926 to 1934, when he was dismissed from his post because of his Jewish ancestry. Oppel’s disparaging remarks about his compositions may reflect more the envy of a younger colleague’s success than any purported straying from “natural” laws of music: Raphael’s prodigious output includes a cello concerto in D minor, an oboe sonata in B minor, and string quartets in E minor and C major that were premiered by the Busch Quartet. einem Protegé Straube’s; merkwürdig, wie die jungen Leute alle der Natur möglichst aus dem Wege gehn.

Übrigens wird die Bläserserenade um einen Tag verschoben, also erst Mittwoch nacht auf Donnerstag gespielt, 030–130.The broadcast was originally planned for Tuesday September 16, as recorded in Oppel’s letter of September 11 (OJ 13/10, [111]).
I am sending you here the six piano pieces for my wife and Ulrich; but please do not spend more than a quarter of an hour on them. No. 6 comes with a special greeting to your dear wife. –
Business will not begin in earnest until tomorrow. The newly arrived crop of students is not worth much. – At present, the talk of the day is of the election results. –

Afterwards, I shall start work on the instrumentation and revision of my Passacaglia. – I saw and played new compositions by my colleague Raphael, a protégé of Straube; it is striking to see how all the young people go as far as possible away from nature’s path.
By the way, the broadcast of my Wind Serenade has been postponed by a day; that is, it will not be played until Wednesday/Thursday night, from 12:30 to 1:30.

tei:closer.tei:salute: All good wishes and the most cordial
greetings to the two of you!
